name: authority_cleanup_seam_cutover
outcome_goal: Cut dispatch, compiler, and verification seams over to canonical DB authority so the runtime no longer fabricates registry resolution or route selection in memory.
anti_requirements: Do not leave silent resolver fabrication in place.
Do not reintroduce module allowlists as code-owned runtime truth.
Do not widen into integration backfill work.

jobs.label: seam_cutover_packet
jobs.outcome_goal: Replace runtime-fabricated authority with DB-loaded authority across the core seams.
jobs.anti_vibe_rule: No compatibility theater; missing authority must fail visibly.
jobs.prompt:
OBJECTIVE:
Cut the core runtime seams over to DB-loaded authority.

TASK:
Replace fabricated dispatch resolver state, hardcoded compiler route hints, and manifest/module allowlists with DB-loaded authority surfaces. The runtime must stop inventing fallback resolver rows or route catalogs in memory. Missing rows must become explicit failures with clear error paths.

CONSTRAINTS:
Leave UI implementation loaders alone. Do not perform integration backfills or registry seeding here. Do not use environment or shell defaults as substitutes for admitted authority rows.

OUTPUT_CONTRACT:
Update the core runtime seams and their tests so DB-backed authority is the only runtime-selectable source for resolver and route behavior.

ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA:
- Dispatch no longer fabricates `RegistryResolver` in memory.
- Compiler and manifest routing no longer depend on hardcoded selection tables.
- Failure paths are explicit when authority is missing.

STOP_BOUNDARY:
Do not tackle integration metadata, provider probe defaults, or workflow discovery in this packet.
